assessments.status_rationale: The prediction is about the BLS CPI report for May 2026 (released by 2026-06-15). Today’s news references inflation fears tied to the Iran war/cease-fire and consumer sentiment, but provides no May 2026 CPI print or BLS release confirming ≥3.6% y/y.

News includes Iran war/cease-fire and implications for gas prices/airfares, but there is no report that any listed major European hub airport publicly confirmed jet-fuel supply constraints causing uplift limits or ≥50 departures canceled in a 72-hour window explicitly linked to Middle East conflict/shipping disruption.

The claim concerns TSCL (or comparable estimator) publishing a 2027 Social Security COLA estimate ≥3.8% by 2026-08-31. Today’s bullets include a Social Security benefits Q&A, but no COLA estimate publication meeting the stated threshold and source criteria.
This requires TSMC reporting Q2 2026 revenue up ≥25% y/y by 2026-07-10. Today’s news set contains no TSMC monthly revenue release or Q2 aggregation data.

The prediction requires an explicit citation by Walmart/Target/Home Depot/Lowe’s (earnings call or release) that higher gasoline/energy prices are a material headwind to discretionary demand. While there are general articles about gas prices and inflation fears, there is no qualifying retailer earnings quote or disclosure in the provided news.

This concerns public guidance from at least two U.S. bank regulators explicitly mentioning 'generative AI' or 'GenAI' by 2026-11-30. Today’s items include market commentary on banks and AI-industry event coverage, but no regulator-issued guidance documents meeting the requirement.
The event is CoreWeave publicly filing an IPO registration statement on SEC EDGAR by 2026-12-31. No EDGAR filing evidence or related reporting is included in today’s bullets.

The prediction requires a French central-government digital authority roadmap for Windows-to-Linux migration with dated milestones (2027 or earlier) and at least three named ministries/agencies by 2026-10-31. Today’s news includes some open-source/tech links but no official French government roadmap publication fitting these criteria.

This requires an EU national government announcement of a central-government desktop migration initiative to Linux/open-source OS citing digital sovereignty/reduced U.S. vendor dependence by 2026-12-31. No such government announcement appears in today’s bullets.
